correctResponse:
The **2015 Denver Broncos** are cemented in NFL history for fielding one of the most dominant and statistically significant defenses of the modern era. Led by defensive coordinator Wade Phillips, this unit—affectionately known as the **"No-Fly Zone"**—practically dragged the team to a victory in Super Bowl 50.

### The Greatness of the 2015 Denver Broncos
While the offense struggled during Peyton Manning’s final season, the defense was a historically elite juggernaut.

* **Statistical Dominance:** They finished the season ranked **#1 in total defense**, #1 in pass defense, and #1 in sacks (52). 
* **The Postseason Gauntlet:** Their path to the title was legendary. They defeated three of the best quarterbacks of the era in consecutive games: Ben Roethlisberger (Steelers), Tom Brady (Patriots), and the league MVP Cam Newton (Panthers).
* **The "No-Fly Zone":** The secondary featured a trio of All-Pros: Aqib Talib, Chris Harris Jr., and T.J. Ward. They were so effective that opposing teams often abandoned the deep passing game entirely.
* **The Pass Rush:** Von Miller and DeMarcus Ware provided a terrifying edge rush. In Super Bowl 50, Miller recorded 2.5 sacks and two forced fumbles, earning Super Bowl MVP honors.

---

### Facts about Darren Sharper
It’s important to clarify a few things regarding **Darren Sharper**. While he was a high-profile safety, his legacy in the NFL and his public standing are complicated by severe criminal convictions.

#### On the Field (1997–2010)
Sharper was a prolific "ball-hawk" safety known more for his interceptions and range than for being the "hardest hitter" (a title usually reserved for contemporaries like Sean Taylor, Brian Dawkins, or Ed Reed).
* **The "Ball-Hawk":** He ranks **6th all-time in NFL history** with **63 career interceptions**.
* **Return Specialist:** He holds the NFL record for most interception return yards in a single season (376 in 2009) and is tied for the most defensive touchdowns in history (13).
* **Accolades:** He was a 5-time Pro Bowler, 2-time First-team All-Pro, and a member of the NFL 2000s All-Decade Team.

#### Off the Field and Legal Status
Sharper’s football achievements have been entirely overshadowed by his life after the game. 
* **Criminal Convictions:** In 2016, Sharper was sentenced to **20 years in prison** after pleading guilty or no contest to drugging and raping several women across four states (California, Arizona, Nevada, and Louisiana).
* **Hall of Fame Status:** Despite his statistical qualifications, his convictions have effectively ended any chance of him being inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame; while he is technically eligible, he has never progressed past the initial nomination phase.

> **Note:** If you were thinking of a "hardest hitting" safety specifically from that 2015 Broncos era, you might be thinking of **T.J. Ward**, who was the enforcer of the No-Fly Zone and known for his punishing, physical style of play.
